revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24755395338|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Inamura-jinja (Hitachiota)]][[ja:稲村神社 (常陸太田市)]] {{Expand Japanese|稲村神社 (常陸太田市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Inamura Shrine | native_name = 稲村神社 | image = Inamura-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Inamura Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|36.527671|140.501066}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|fr|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|simple|Nigihayahi|zh|饒速日命|qid=Q10949054|1=Nigihayahi}} }} {{nihongo|'''Inamura Shrine'''|稲村神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Hitachi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11481683|ltq=Q11481683|qid=Q11481683|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 11 ** P155: {{ill|Q11663648|ltq=Q11663648|qid=Q11663648|1=Shizu Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Tachinono Shrine|lt=Tachinono Shrine|qid=Q135039763|1=Tachinono Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q838663|ltq=Q838663|qid=Q838663|1=Hitachiōta, Ibaraki}} * {{ill|Hitachi Province|lt=Hitachi Province|cs|Provincie Hitači|de|Provinz Hitachi|en|Hitachi Province|es|Provincia de Hitachi|fa|ولایت هیتاچی|fr|Province de Hitachi|gl|Provincia de Hitachi|hak|Hitachi-koet|id|Provinsi Hitachi|it|Hitachi (provincia)|ja|常陸国|ko|히타치국|lt|Hitačio žemė|nl|Hitachi (provincie)|pt|Província de Hitachi|ru|Хитати (провинция)|simple|Hitachi Province|sk|Hitači (provincia)|sr|Провинција Хитачи|tr|Hitachi (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Хітаті|zh_min_nan|Hitati-kok|zh_yue|常陸國|zh|常陸國|qid=Q1055353|1=Hitachi Province}} * {{ill|Q1075212|ltq=Q1075212|qid=Q1075212|1=Kuji District, Ibaraki}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|36.527671|140.501066}} == image (P18) == * Inamura-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q10949054|ltq=Q10949054|qid=Q10949054|1=Nigihayahi}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181764 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Gō-sha|lt=Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11545372|ltq=Q11545372|qid=Q11545372|1=Senior Sixth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q11071125|ltq=Q11071125|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == legal form (P1454) == * {{ill|Q7311343|ltq=Q7311343|qid=Q7311343|1=religious corporation}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|es|Shinmeizukuri|fr|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|ru|Симмэй-дзукури|tr|Shinmei-zukuri|zh|神明造|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いなむらじんじゃhttps://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == postal code (P281) == * 313-0049https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8050005008175 == Commons category (P373) == * Inamura-jinja (Hitachiota) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039762|ltq=Q135039762|qid=Q135039762|1=Inamurano Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '茨城県常陸太田市天神林町3228', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Inamura-jinja (Hitachiota)}} [[JA:稲村神社 (常陸太田市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17193413}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Inamura Shrine |Image = [[File:Inamura-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|qid=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hitachiōta City|Q838663|qid=Q838663|1=Hitachiōta, Ibaraki}} Tenjinbayashi-chō 3228 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|qid=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor) {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of the 13th Emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|qid=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}} |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Other names = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|qid=Q2544|1=May 1}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Inamura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Inamura-jinja (Hitachiota) ninotorii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Second torii gate}}]] '''Inamura Shrine''' (いなむらじんじゃ, 稻村神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tenjinbayashi-chō, {{ill|lt=Hitachiōta City|Q838663|qid=Q838663|1=Hitachiōta, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|qid=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines the following 12 deities: * {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|qid=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} – ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|qid=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}. * 11 deities of the {{ill|lt=Seven Generations of the Age of the Gods|Q3275893|qid=Q3275893|1=Kamiyonanayo}} === History === === Foundation === [[File:Bontenyama-kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bontenyama Kofun|1=UNKNOWN}}}}{{small|According to one tradition, the tomb of Funase no Sukune, the first {{ill|lt=Kuji no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11369664|qid=Q11369664|1=Kuji Kokuzō}}.}}]] It is widely believed that the area where Inamura Shrine stands was the stronghold of the {{ill|lt=Kuji no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11369664|qid=Q11369664|1=Kuji Kokuzō}} before the {{ill|lt=ritsuryō system|Q742460|qid=Q742460|1=Ritsuryō}}. According to the “{{ill|lt=Kokuzō Hongi|Q133833194|qid=Q133833194|1=Kokuzo Hongi}}” section of the {{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|qid=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}Sendai Kuji Hongi, “Kokuzō Hongi,” entry on Kuji no Kuni no Miyatsuko., during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|qid=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}, Funase no Sukune, a third-generation descendant of Ikashikoo no Mikoto (a distant ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|qid=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe no Muraji|1=Mononobe clan}}), was appointed as the first Kuji no Kuni no Miyatsuko. Shrine tradition holds that at the time of this appointment, Funase no Sukune enshrined his ancestral deity {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|qid=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}. Because a “heavenly deity” or seven sacred mirrors were enshrined, the shrine came to be called “Seven-Generation Tenjin” {{Sfn|Inamura Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}. Another tradition says that when {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} campaigned eastward, he enshrined the spirits of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Seven Generations of the Heavenly Deities|1=UNKNOWN}} at this site {{Sfn|Inamura Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}. To the southwest of Inamura Shrine lies the {{ill|lt=Bontenyama Kofun Cluster|Q17220423|qid=Q17220423|1=Bontenyama-kofungun}}. Its main tumulus, Bontenyama Kofun, is the second largest in Ibaraki Prefecture, and there is a tradition identifying it as the tomb of Funase no Sukune“Bontenyama Kofun Cluster,” Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 8: Place Names of Ibaraki Prefecture, Heibonsha, 1982.. === Overview === In the {{ill|lt=Six National Histories|Q1151168|qid=Q1151168|lt=official histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, an entry states that in the second year of {{ill|lt=Kashō|Q1373157|qid=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} ({{ill|lt=849|Q23902|qid=Q23902|1=849}}) “Inamura no Kami” was placed under official offerings as a government shrine and displayed miraculous powers in times of flood and drought {{Sfn|Inamura Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}. Later, in the second year of {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|qid=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} ({{ill|lt=878|Q23910|qid=Q23910|1=878}}), its {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|qid=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} was raised to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and in the first year of {{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|qid=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} ({{ill|lt=885|Q24894|qid=Q24894|1=885}}) it was further promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=register of shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in the fifth year of {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), it is listed as “Inamura Shrine” in {{ill|lt=Kuji District|Q1075212|qid=Q1075212|1=Kuji District, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|qid=Q1055353|1=Hitachi Province}}, and ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. However, there are other {{ill|lt=candidate shrines|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for this “Inamura no Kami”These include {{ill|lt=Chikatsu Shrine (Shimonogu, Daigo Town)|Q54153265|qid=Q54153265|1=Chikata Shrine (Kanda)}} in Daigo Town, Kuji District, Ibaraki Prefecture, and {{ill|lt=Isobe Inamura Shrine|Q65681696|qid=Q65681696|1=Isobe inamura Shrine}} in Sakuragawa City, Ibaraki Prefecture. The former is thought to be located in Mutsu Province, and the latter, while in Hitachi Province, is not in Kuji District.. According to the *Newly Compiled Gazetteer of Hitachi Province*, when {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Q1194944|qid=Q1194944|1=Tokugawa Mitsukuni}} passed through Tenjinbayashi Village in the fourth year of {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|qid=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} ({{ill|lt=1691|Q7682|qid=Q7682|1=1691}}), he inquired about the locations of the seven Tenjin shrines and inspected seven mounds then known collectively as “Seven-Generation Tenjin.” He advised that they be unified into a single shrine, and in the sixth year of Genroku ({{ill|lt=1693|Q7689|qid=Q7689|1=1693}}) the shrine buildings were constructed at the present location and the deities enshrined together {{Sfn|Inamura Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}. Mitsukuni also had a plaque reading “Seven-Generation Tenjingū” hung on the torii gate and dedicated sacred treasures {{Sfn|Inamura Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}. During the Edo period, the shrine held 6 koku and 5 to of shrine land, and 9 koku, 9 to, and 3 shō as tax-exempt land {{Sfn|Inamura Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine was identified with the Shikinaisha Inamura Shrine and its name was changed from “Seven-Generation Tenjingū”[Archived link] Inamura Shrine (Research on Engishiki Shrines, personal website).. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Kashō|Q1373157|qid=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=849|Q23902|qid=Q23902|1=849}}), April 7: placed under official offerings as a government shrine (*{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|qid=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}*)Shoku Nihon Kōki, Kashō 2 (849), April, day kōin (7). – Recorded as “Inamura no Kami (稻村神).” * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|qid=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=878|Q23910|qid=Q23910|1=878}}), August 23: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*)Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku, Gangyō 2 (878), August 23. – Recorded as “Inamura no Kami (稻村神).” * {{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|qid=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} 1 ({{ill|lt=885|Q24894|qid=Q24894|1=885}}), May 22: promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (*Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku*)Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku, Ninna 1 (885), May 22. – Recorded as “Inamura no Kami (稻村神).” === Shrine grounds === File:Inamura-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|Main hall File:Inamura-jinja (Hitachiota) ichinotorii.JPG|First torii gate === On-site information === '''Address''' * Tenjinbayashi-chō 3228, {{ill|lt=Hitachiōta City|Q838663|qid=Q838663|1=Hitachiōta, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|qid=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}} '''Nearby sites''' * {{ill|lt=Satake-ji|Q11383389|qid=Q11383389|1=Satake-ji}} * {{ill|lt=Bontenyama Kofun Cluster|Q17220423|qid=Q17220423|1=Bontenyama-kofungun}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} '''Annotations''' {{reflist|group="note"}} '''Primary sources''' {{reflist|group="original"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * On-site explanatory plaque * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=Inamura Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|qid=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 8: Place Names of Ibaraki Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490085|ref={{Harvid|Inamura Shrine (Heibonsha)|1982}}}} === Related articles === {{Commonscat|Inamura-jinja (Hitachiota)|Inamura Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kuji no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11369664|qid=Q11369664|1=Kuji Kokuzō}} {{Shinto footer}} {{List of shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Inamura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Inamura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Hitachiōta City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hitachiota]]
